description: Cross-sector baseline compliance controls for the United Kingdom v1. Rules are evidence-driven and deterministic; absence of evidence does not by itself constitute legal non-compliance.

metadata.scope: UK baseline controls for document/document-set review.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official UK legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.
metadata.territorial_scope_note: UK law can differ across England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. This starter pack uses UK-wide sources where available and flags devolved or nation-specific application for human review.

primary_sources.name: UK GDPR guidance: lawful basis, controllers and processors
Network and Information Systems Regulations and UK cybersecurity guidance
Money Laundering, Terrorist Financing and Transfer of Funds Regulations 2017

rules.title: Privacy notice or transparency information evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks whether the document contains privacy notice, transparency, or personal-data disclosure evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Lawful basis and processing purpose evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks whether the document explains why personal data is processed and identifies a lawful basis or equivalent processing ground.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Data-subject rights channel evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for data-subject rights, rights-request handling, complaints, or ICO escalation evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Controller, processor, or data-sharing governance evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for evidence of controller/processor allocation, processor contract, joint-controller arrangement, or third-party sharing controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Information-security safeguards evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for technical and organisational security controls, access controls, encryption, authentication, or incident-response language.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: AML risk assessment or customer due diligence evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for AML, CDD, beneficial ownership, screening, suspicious activity, or risk-assessment evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Consumer disclosure, complaint, or redress evidence is present where consumer-facing
rules.summary: Checks for customer communications, complaint handling, redress, fair treatment, or consumer-support evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Corporate identity, registration, or statutory filing evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for Companies House, company number, registered office, annual accounts, confirmation statement, or statutory filing evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
